inputs.prompt: image1 is a sprite sheet strip of four grey mannequins in a row, four frames of one walk cycle animation. Draw the character from image2 four times, once per frame, each copy in the exact pose and camera angle of the mannequin in that frame. Keep the character identical in every frame. The character must face the same direction as the mannequin: facing the viewer, face and both eyes clearly visible. Full body, whole figure visible, feet on the same ground line as the mannequin, soft even lighting, warm highlights, no shadow on the ground, Change background to solid Green color
inputs.instruction: Describe the character and their key features (body shape, physical characteristics, clothing, items, accessories). Then explain how the user's text instruction should alter or modify the character. Generate a new image that meets the user's requirements while maintaining consistency with the original character where appropriate.

inputs.prompt: image1 is a sprite sheet strip of four grey mannequins in a row, four frames of one walk cycle animation. Draw the character from image2 four times, once per frame, each copy in the exact pose and camera angle of the mannequin in that frame. Keep the character identical in every frame. The character must face the same direction as the mannequin: in strict left profile, facing the left edge of the image, nose pointing left. Full body, whole figure visible, feet on the same ground line as the mannequin, soft even lighting, warm highlights, no shadow on the ground, Change background to solid Green color

inputs.prompt: image1 is a sprite sheet strip of four grey mannequins in a row, four frames of one walk cycle animation. Draw the character from image2 four times, once per frame, each copy in the exact pose and camera angle of the mannequin in that frame. Keep the character identical in every frame. The character must face the same direction as the mannequin: facing away from the viewer, showing the back of the head and back, no face visible. Full body, whole figure visible, feet on the same ground line as the mannequin, soft even lighting, warm highlights, no shadow on the ground, Change background to solid Green color

_meta.name: walk_cycle_vnccs_ashcaller
_meta.variant_of: walk_cycle_vnccs.json (same models, gait keyframes, mannequin canvases, prompts, post-chain; see its _meta for gait angles, axis semantics and all lessons)

gait_keyframes_deg.axis_semantics: thigh/upperarm rz-=forward swing (same sign both sides); calf rz+=knee bend; foot rz+=plantarflex; upperarm_l rx-/upperarm_r rx+=adduct from A-pose; lowerarm rz-=elbow bend; spine_02 rz-=forward lean; mirror = swap _l/_r, negate rx, keep rz
gait_keyframes_deg.note: contact_B/passing_B are sagittal mirrors; down/up rows use flat feet (foot rz=0) — foot pitch cues made QIE draw tiptoe/floating feet in front/back views

_meta.mannequins: REUSED VERBATIM from the Warden run (blessed by work order): offline corrected-units render, eye cues, flat feet on down/up rows, cell 512x1024, canvas 2048x1024; male-mesh mannequins drive pose/scale only - her build comes from the identity refs

identity_refs.prep: prep_id_ref_v2: bbox-crop, boot-safe baked-shadow scrub v2 (ink-chain protection from above 80% band, <=6-row gaps, +-1px/row drift, seeds above-band only; the v1 red-fleck color rule erased her BROWN boot interiors - median rgb 92,54,49 matches (r>g+30)&(r>b+30)&(r<170), the same failure family as walk scrub v1 eating the Warden's near-black boots), column-support kill, hard alpha, flatten onto green, h=1024

sources.down: EmberMender/Neutral sprite_pose_0001 (front; view seed 20260715 single-view reseed)
sources.left: EmberMender/Neutral sprite_pose_0006 (true left profile, nose left; batch seed 20260714)
sources.right: EmberMender/Neutral sprite_pose_0003 (true right profile, nose right; batch seed 20260714 - usable this time because rim-light words were banned; no mirror hack, satchel anatomy correct)

_meta.post_chain: chroma key (green dominance g-max(r,b)>40 & g>90, plus weak-green rule; despill g clamp) -> ground-shadow scrub v2.1 (rows below 78% height: kill dark/desaturated/green-dark/red-fleck px unless chained to above-band ink — protection propagates downward through kept ink with <=6-row vertical gaps and +-1 px/row horizontal drift; hard cut below 92%. v1 brightness-based sole protection ate the clothed Warden's near-black boot bottoms; QIE also paints shadow blobs up to ~80% height, above the old 82.5% band start, where they were immune and seeded their own tails) -> fixed 4-column frame cut (never per-frame re-centering) -> per-direction registration: shared baseline=median full-res feet row, shared x-center=mean centroid, ONE global scale (tallest direction char height -> 24px) -> area-coverage downsample into 24x32 box (opaque if >=35% cell coverage, per-channel median color of opaque src px), hard alpha, largest-component filter -> shared integer baseline snap (lowest frame feet -> row 29) -> per-direction mediancut quantize <=64 colors (fg only)
_meta.frame_box: 24x32, feet baseline row 29, char height ~22-24px (style-bible sprite_px_height 24); sheet rows down/left/right/up, cols contactA/passingA/contactB/passingB
_meta.run_record: 2026-07-12: facing test 1 frame/direction all correct first try at the Warden-pinned seeds; all 4 batch canvases green first try (~33s each); post-chain v2.1 unchanged -> gate ALL GREEN first pass, 0 repair rounds, 0 reseeds. Identity-ref prep repaired 1x (deterministic boot-safe v2, not a candidate burn).
_meta.lessons: front/back rows: keep mannequin feet FLAT (pitch cues become tiptoes); facing flips are seed-luck — reseed beats prompt escalation; do chroma+shadow removal offline, QIE will not reliably omit ground shadows even when told to; dark clothing breaks brightness-based sole protection — protect by ink connectivity from above the scrub band instead; QIE paints ground-shadow blobs higher than the ground line, so the scrub band must start above them || NEW (Ashcaller): color-rule scrubs break on every new outfit palette - brown boots died to the red-fleck rule exactly as near-black boots died to brightness rules; ink-chain geometry is the only outfit-agnostic protection. True right profiles are usable when rim-light words are banned at the clothes stage (no mirror hack, correct satchel anatomy).
